## Discussion

This function consumes the app’s API calls since the last [doc://com.apple.phase/documentation/PHASE/PHASEEngine/update()], adjusts internal systems, objects, increments parameters, and invokes the app’s queued callbacks. An API call may require several [doc://com.apple.phase/documentation/PHASE/PHASEEngine/update()] invocations before the output device reflects the call’s results.

The framework ignores calls to this function for engines with `updateMode` set to [doc://com.apple.phase/documentation/PHASE/PHASEEngine/UpdateMode/automatic]; for more more information, see [doc://com.apple.phase/documentation/PHASE/PHASEEngine/init(updateMode:)].

Call [doc://com.apple.phase/documentation/PHASE/PHASEEngine/update()] at a rate that matches your app’s visuals or logic update rate for optimal performance:

This function offers thread safety for apps that intend to call [doc://com.apple.phase/documentation/PHASE/PHASEEngine/update()] off of the main thread.

# update()

**Instance Method**

Processes app commands and increments framework processing.

## Declaration

```swift
func update()
```

## Discussion

This function consumes the app’s API calls since the last [doc://com.apple.phase/documentation/PHASE/PHASEEngine/update()], adjusts internal systems, objects, increments parameters, and invokes the app’s queued callbacks. An API call may require several [doc://com.apple.phase/documentation/PHASE/PHASEEngine/update()] invocations before the output device reflects the call’s results.

The framework ignores calls to this function for engines with `updateMode` set to [doc://com.apple.phase/documentation/PHASE/PHASEEngine/UpdateMode/automatic]; for more more information, see [doc://com.apple.phase/documentation/PHASE/PHASEEngine/init(updateMode:)].



### Update an Engine Manually

On an engine configured for manual updates ([doc://com.apple.phase/documentation/PHASE/PHASEEngine/UpdateMode/manual]), call this function periodically to instruct the framework to process API calls and perform internal updates. Call [doc://com.apple.phase/documentation/PHASE/PHASEEngine/update()] at a rate that matches your app’s visuals or logic update rate for optimal performance:

- Apps that process graphics at 60 FPS can invoke [doc://com.apple.phase/documentation/PHASE/PHASEEngine/update()] in their display link callback.
- Rates in the range of 240Hz to 30Hz offer equivalent audio performance, however apps that actively change the parameters of playing audio achieve smoother interpolation at a higher rate.
- If a game skips frames due to long running graphics routines, an app can throttle [doc://com.apple.phase/documentation/PHASE/PHASEEngine/update()] calls to values less than 30Hz without affecting audio quality as long as the system isn’t overloaded.

This function offers thread safety for apps that intend to call [doc://com.apple.phase/documentation/PHASE/PHASEEngine/update()] off of the main thread.

## Controlling and Inspecting Playback State

- **pause()**: Pauses all audio playback.
- **start()**: Starts or resumes all audio playback.
- **stop()**: Stops all audio playback.
- **renderingState**: The status of the engine’s audio playback.
- **lastRenderTime**
